title: Towards Efficient Monitoring of Slowly Evolving Degradation Processes: Insights on Feedback Mechanisms in Future Neural Network
abstract: Monitoring evolving degradation processes of systems, particularly in heavy industries such as mechanical and rotating machinery, is crucial for ensuring reliability and efficiency. However, accurate monitoring faces significant challenges due to unavailability of realistic data, often constrained by maintenance schedules, along with data drift and complexity. Data drift arises from dynamically changing working conditions and variations in health states due to factors such as damage propagation, aging, fatigue, and other evolving operational and environmental influences. Complexity, on the other hand, stems from the semi-random nature of recorded data under such conditions, making traditional analysis methods like signal processing and statistical analysis less effective in handling large, dynamic signals. This work primarily addresses these challenges, focusing on both data drift and complexity, while introducing an additional perspective on incorporating feedback mechanisms and error correction for improved adaptability. This is particularly relevant in the context of neural networks and deep learning, which are at the forefront of state-of-the-art monitoring techniques. Using a simulated synthetic dataset that emulates realistic degradation behaviors, we illustrate and analyze these key aspects. No preprocessing techniques were introduced to ensure that the effect of the feedback mechanism was assessed separately. The results demonstrate that incorporating feedback mechanisms leads to measurable improvements, with an increase in $\mathrm{R}^{\mathrm{2}}$ of approximately $\mathrm{1. 6 9 \%}$ for training and $\mathrm{1. 8 2 \%}$ for testing. This highlights the significance of feedback mechanisms in enhancing monitoring accuracy and underscores their inclusion in future research, whether in deep learning methods or small-scale machine learning systems.

title: Development of Educational Applications with ABB GoFa Collaborative Robot using Externally Guided Motion
abstract: In academics and in the engineering field in particular, theoretical knowledge is best complemented by handson practice sessions from which students can largely benefit to retain the information learned in the classroom. Robotics is a multidisciplinary science that inevitably falls into this category: once the underlying mathematics are established, it is best to see the mechanism in action. A variety of complementary materials can be included in university courses on robotics to aid students in better understanding the task at hand. This work presents a practical approach in this regard, showcasing a collection of applications developed for the ABB GoFa collaborative robot: visual servoing, 3D surface reconstruction, path correction using force-torque sensor feedback, and teleoperation using low-cost devices and virtual reality headsets. The Externally Guided Motion feature is at the core of the proposed common architecture. The applications were tested either in the RobotStudio simulator or on the real robot.

title: Low computational cost algorithm for harmonic elimination in multilevel converters
abstract: This paper presents an improved selective harmonic elimination procedure for L-level cascaded H-bridge converters operating at the fundamental switching frequency. The procedure is subject to the constraint that $l=2 \cdot 2^{n}+1$, where n is an integer. Previously, the unknown switching angles were obtained using an analytical formula and the arccos function. The new method replaces the arccos function with a Maclaurin series truncated at the third term. This modification offers the following advantages: (i) much less computational effort; (ii) the absence of truncation errors; (iii) the ability to eliminate low order n harmonics and their multiples from the output voltage waveform, while keeping the fundamental fixed; (iv) low total harmonic distortion with a wide range of modulation indices. In some cases, the procedure mitigates other low order harmonics, in addition to selected harmonics elimination, to meet standard code requirements. The algorithm was successfully simulated for a three-phase inverter assiming $l=5,9$, and 17 levels.

title: Optimal Sizing and Energy Management for Multi-Energy Microgrids Integrated with Combined Heat and Power Unit
abstract: This paper presents an optimization study on the sizing of a Multi-Energy Microgrid (MEMG), comprising a connected DC microgrid that integrates wind turbines, photovoltaic (PV) panels, battery storage, Combined Heat and Power (CHP) unit, and multiple electrical loads. Additionally, heat storage, CHP unit, and direct gas supply are employed to meet heating demands. To determine the optimal sizing, two primary objectives are defined: first, to maximize the utilization of renewable energy sources, including PV, wind, and CHP; and second, to minimize the total costs, which include electricity, natural gas, emissions, and operational expenses. The influence of key design parameters-such as PV surface area, wind turbine capacity, and gas allocation to CHP-on various cost components is assessed across 51 distinct scenarios. Subsequently, heat and power management strategies are applied to three selected scenarios, each representing a distinctly different situation, and the results are compared. The optimization problem is modeled as a MixedInteger Nonlinear Programming (MINLP) problem and solved using the BARON solver within the GAMS environment.

title: Indicators for Maintenance Management: Application and Results in a Production Workshop
abstract: This article presents an analysis of the main performance indicators and fault surface (SDF) that can be extracted from the results of a simulation conducted on a production system. These indicators, which are essential for monitoring the performance of the production tool, help to highlight maintenance issues, support strategic decision-making and measure improvements after the implementation of new strategies. Through a case study using the Alosurf software, we demonstrate how these metrics provide increased visibility on system failures and inefficiencies, while offering opportunities for improvement to optimize maintenance decisions. The results show that rigorous monitoring of these indicators can be a key lever to ensure reliability and productivity of industrial systems.

title: Extracting Geometric Features from CT Images of Walnuts for Precision Agriculture
abstract: Walnuts are the second most consumed nut, with the U.S. accounting for approximately $25 \%$ of global demand. Beyond the food industry, walnut shells can be utilized in durable batteries, cost-effective concrete, and stronger epoxy composites. As climate change and increasing demand impact walnut production, farmers face the challenge of efficiently selecting breeds with desirable traits., such as a high kernel-to-total-weight ratio, appropriate shell thickness, and the ability to easily extract both halves of the kernel intact. In this work, we analyzed walnut CT images obtained from Heli Scan CT to extract geometric features for precision agriculture. Most walnut phenotyping use caliper measurement of features according to International Plant Genetic Resources Institute 1994 guidelines, including kernel-fillratio, sphericity, rugosity, shape. However, as defined, rugosity and shape are mathematically linked to sphericity and not independent. We propose a new automated method to calculate surface roughness by calculating sum-absolute-difference from a “mean shape” found using Fourier Transform techniques. We also extract the standard features using automated methods. Our ultimate goal is to correlate these features with promising walnut genotypes and to automate the selection process through machine learning.

title: Design of Fixed-Time Sliding Mode Control for Chaotic Dynamics with Uncertainties and Disturbances
abstract: This article addresses the control of chaotic systems with uncertainties, including external disturbances, using sliding mode control (SMC). To control these systems, an autonomous differential equation is introduced to ensure that, regardless of initial conditions, all solution trajectories converge to zero within a predetermined fixed time, adjustable through a specific parameter. Utilizing this differential equation, a sliding surface is defined to control the system’s chaotic dynamics. When system trajectories deviate from this surface, the proposed control strategies are designed to bring them back onto it within finite time, ensuring fixed-time stability. This approach, which incorporates a robust sliding mode framework, guarantees that the trajectories converge to the desired state regardless of external perturbations and uncertainties. A lemma, two corollaries, and three theorems rigorously demonstrate the fixed-time stability of the proposed method. Additionally, the results are shown to be applicable to chaotic systems that are both time-dependent and timeindependent, broadening the applicability of the control strategy. To illustrate the method’s effectiveness, two case studies are provided. The first example examines the timedependent Gyro system, with simulation results compared to prior research. The second example investigates the timeindependent Liu system, similarly validated against existing work. This approach provides a robust solution for controlling chaotic systems with adjustable convergence times and minimal chattering, essential in practical applications involving dynamic systems.

title: Biomechanical Analysis of Spine and Shoulder Compensation in a User of an Ultrasound-Based Human–Machine Interface for Hand Control
abstract: Upper limb amputation often leads to compensatory movement patterns that increase the risk of overuse injuries in the shoulder and spine. This work highlights the urgent need for a refined model of compensatory strategies, particularly how prosthetic degree of freedom (DoF) restrictions influence movement distribution across the shoulder and spine. Such a model is crucial for informing the design and control of nextgeneration prostheses that minimize compensatory burden and improve user outcomes. A male participant with trans-radial amputation - who routinely uses a simple on/off myoelectric prosthesis was fitted with a custom socket and a prosthetic controlled by a 32-channel A-mode ultrasound interface, and performed the Clothespin Relocation Test. Motion capture and surface electromyography were used to assess shoulder and trunk compensation across three wrist configurations: restricted supination/pronation, flexion/extension, and a combined restriction. Limiting supination/pronation at the wrist resulted in greater shoulder range of motion and lumbar muscle activity, while flexion/extension restrictions led to increased thoracic spine involvement. The combined supination/pronation and flexion/extension at the wrist joint reduced the total compensation but shifted the load to the nondominant shoulder. These results highlight the adaptable nature of the musculoskeletal system and underscore the importance of optimizing wrist DoFs in prosthetic design. Findings also inform rehabilitation strategies by identifying high-risk compensatory zones, offering guidance to improve prosthetic function and reduce long-term musculoskeletal injury.

title: Optimal Sliding Mode Control for Differential Drive Mobile Robots
abstract: This paper presents a robust trajectory tracking controller for differential drive mobile robots based on First-OrderSliding Mode Control (FOSMC). Unlike advanced sliding mode variants that rely on higher-order dynamics or complex surface formulations, FOSMC offers a simple yet effective control structure with strong robustness against modeling uncertainties and external disturbances. However, its performance in terms of response time and chattering reduction depends heavily on the appropriate tuning of its control parameters. To address this, we employ a parameter optimization strategy using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to automatically determine the optimal gains of the sliding mode controller. The proposed approach enhances the controller’s responsiveness and minimizes steady-state tracking errors while preserving the low computational cost and structural simplicity of FOSMC. Simulation results confirm that this optimized FOSMC design achieves superior performance in terms of convergence speed and control smoothness, offering a practical and efficient alternative to more complex SMC formulations that require higher computational or hardware resources.

title: Evaluation of Toothbrush Quality Using AI-based Virtual Tooth Plaque Assessment
abstract: As oral health issues are becoming increasingly important, how to measure tooth stain and evaluate the toothbrush cleaning effect has become an important research topic. In this paper, AI-based virtual tartar assessment method is proposed. YOLOv10 target detection integrated with Otsu threshold segmentation is proposed for automatic measurement of tooth stain. First, the YOLOv10 target detection algorithm is used to automatically locate and extract tooth regions from the input image to ensure accurate focusing on the relevant regions. Then Otsu threshold segmentation method are used to segment the tooth surface and extract stains. Finally, the tooth cleaning effect is estimated by calculating the area variation of the stained regions before and after cleaning. Experiments conducted on the collected real cases show that the method has high accuracy and robustness in tooth stain detection and toothbrush cleaning ability evaluation. This study can provide technical support for evaluating the effectiveness of dental cleaning products and personalised oral care programmes.

title: Circularly Polarized Multi-Antenna System Using UHF Backscattering for Indoor Localization
abstract: This manuscript presents an ultra-high frequency (UHF) multi-antenna system for indoor localization. The multiantenna system is made up of four artificial magnetic conductor (AMC)-backed antenna elements that rotate in sequence. Each element consists of an antenna supported by a $3 \times 3$ AMC reflector surface. The AMC-supported antenna has an impedance bandwidth of $84 \mathrm{MHz}(837-921 \mathrm{MHz})$. The antenna portrays right-hand circular polarization (RHCP) in the 851-882 MHz frequency range. The multi-antenna system has interelement isolation of greater than 20 dB. The addition of the AMC surface results in a 6.96 dB peak gain enhancement, which leads to better read ranges in indoor positioning environments. These characteristics make the suggested multi-antenna system wellsuited to indoor localization scenarios.

title: Fuzzy-Based Gain Tuning of a Super-Twisting Controller for Trajectory Tracking in Autonomous Underwater Vehicles
abstract: Underwater vehicles frequently operate in highly uncertain and dynamically changing environments, making robust and efficient control strategies imperative. In this paper, we address the tracking control problem for an underwater vehicle subject to external disturbances using a Super-Twisting Controller, which ensures finite-time convergence and robustness. To further mitigate the chattering phenomenon typically associated with such high-order sliding mode controllers, we introduce a fuzzy logic-based tuning scheme that adjusts the controller gains in real time according to a rule derived from the sliding surface. A comparative study with a conventional adaptive scheme and a classical controller in numerical simulations highlights the enhanced performance and reliability of the fuzzy-adaptive supertwisting algorithm, ensuring improved tracking accuracy and reduced control effort.

title: Deep-Shallow Features Fusion for Induction Motor Fault Diagnosis through Infrared Thermography Images
abstract: Accurate fault diagnosis in induction motors (IMs) is critical to ensuring reliable operation. This paper presents a machine learning-based fault diagnosis method using infrared thermography (IRT). The proposed framework integrates a pre-trained deep convolutional neural network (DCNN), MobileNetV2, with a shallow feature extraction method, local binary pattern (LBP), and employs a Support Vector Machine (SVM) for classification. First, MobileNetV2 and LBP extract discriminative features from IRT images. Then, the fault type is identified using an SVM classifier. The proposed model is evaluated on a dataset of thermal images corresponding to five types of induction motor faults. Additionally, image augmentation is applied to enhance data diversity. Experimental results demonstrate that the deep-shallow approach achieves a competitive accuracy of $\mathbf{9 8. 9 7 \%}$, confirming its efficiency and suitability for deployment on low-cost edge computing devices.

title: Navigation State Estimation of a Quadcopter Using Multi-Sensor Data Fusion
abstract: This paper presents the development and application of multisensor noise reduction and fusion filters to estimate the navigation states of a quadcopter using an Arduino Due board. A Kalman filter attenuates noise from the inertial measurement unit (IMU), while a complementary filter integrates accelerometer and gyroscope data to estimate the system’s attitude accurately. A second Kalman filter combines accelerometer predictions with Global Positioning System (GPS) updates to provide a more reliable position estimate. To optimize computational efficiency, a cascaded fusion architecture is implemented, consisting of three parallel Kalman filters, each dedicated to a position axis, and three parallel complementary filters for angular measurements. This architecture reduces computational complexity and supports real-time execution. The filters were developed in MATLAB/Simulink and implemented in Arduino Due using the processor-in-the-loop (PIL) technique. The system demonstrates high accuracy and real-time performance, validating its effectiveness for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) estimation and tracking applications.

title: Embedded Fuzzy Control of a Teleoperation System on Zedboard Zynq-7000 SoC Board
abstract: This paper presents Fuzzy Logic Control (FLC) embedded in a force position teleoperation system. The aim is to design two Takagi-Sugeno fuzzy controllers (position and force) to control the position of the slave and the force feedback of the master. Fuzzy logic, which is a model-free approach, is used to overcome the difficulties encountered in conventional control methods because the master and slave systems are complex and difficult to control. Fuzzy controllers are designed so that the slave system follows the movements of the master system, while the force exerted on the slave by the environment is fed back to the master. The two controllers are then implemented in PIL (Processor In-the-Loop) execution mode via Simulink on an ARM architecture development board (Zedboard) linked to a Zynq-7000 SoC programmable logic. The proposed control strategy was designed in MATLAB’s Simulink environment and then implemented on the Zedboard, based on a Zynq-7000 FPGA and a dual ARM Cortex A-9 processor, in order to evaluate the behaviour of the controllers in PIL mode before their application on the real system. The results obtained were compared with those obtained using PID controllers and showed good tracking and stability performance, highlighting the effectiveness of the control method adopted.

title: AI-Driven Prognostics for Wind Turbine Bearings: Predicting Degradation with Limited Data
abstract: This study predicts wind turbine bearing degradation using artificial intelligence (AI) with limited actual data. Ten health degradation indicators derived from vibration data are evaluated based on metrics such as monotonicity, trendability, and prognosability. The analysis identifies Peak-toRMS (Root Mean Square) as the most effective health indicator for monitoring bearing degradation. The results emphasize the potential of neural networks (NN) and Gaussian Process Regression (GPR) in predicting bearing health in wind turbines with limited data.

title: Bridging the Gap between Prediction and Action: Information Demands and Requirements for a Data-Based Decision Support System for Root Cause Analysis in Production
abstract: Root cause analysis (RCA) is essential for continuously improving manufacturing processes. Increasing product complexity and rising demands on product quality require advanced approaches to RCA in production. Predictive quality models can detect failures in manufacturing processes, but can only determine their causes to a limited extent. This publication therefore investigates information demands for a data-based decision support system for RCA that builds on and extends predictive quality models. For this purpose, a semi-structured expert survey was conducted comprising eleven hypotheses and two open-ended questions derived from established failure analysis methods and the theory of causal reasoning. The results indicate that additional information beyond prediction outcomes is necessary to identify failure causes effectively. Foremost, it is important to understand process parameters and their influence on the product. Additionally, the study identifies requirements for the decision support system, including model independence, stability and consideration of use case and target group. The results of the study provide valuable insights for improving RCA processes through enhanced information provision and system usability in production environments.

title: Enhanced Frame-Matching Algorithm for Image-Based Vehicle Positioning Using Segmented Regions of Interest
abstract: Vehicle positioning is essential for traffic management and advanced functionalities like autonomous driving and platooning. This paper presents an enhanced frame-matching algorithm for image-based vehicle positioning that improves frame-matching accuracy by integrating semantic segmentation using the SegFormer model. A key limitation of previous approaches was the presence of vegetation, which generated numerous irrelevant keypoints, making feature matching unreliable. Additionally, sky regions increased computational complexity by expanding the search space. By filtering out vegetation, terrain, and sky, the method proposed in this paper reduces processing overhead and ensures keypoints are concentrated on meaningful elements such as roads, buildings, and vehicles. This improves feature-matching accuracy and enhances positioning reliability. Experimental results demonstrate the algorithm’s efficiency in diverse real-world scenarios, contributing to the advancement of image-based positioning systems for autonomous technologies and traffic optimization.

title: Advanced Detection of Inter-Turn Short-Circuit Faults in PMSMs Using FFT and AI Methods
abstract: This study introduces a novel method for detecting inter-turn short-circuit (ISF) faults in Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machines (PMSMs). The approach leverages zerosequence voltage signals, identified as highly sensitive indicators of ISF faults, and applies Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) analysis to extract critical features. To automate the fault detection process, Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms including Support Vector Machines (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and decision trees-are employed. Among these, Support Vector Machines (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) demonstrated exceptional performance, achieving perfect precision and recall rates. The integration of signal processing and AI techniques significantly enhances the accuracy and reliability of ISF fault detection. This research represents a meaningful advancement in the development of intelligent monitoring systems for electric machines, supporting early detection and predictive maintenance strategies.

title: Automatic Anomaly Detection in Large Vibration Datasets
abstract: Modern condition monitoring systems enable collection of large vibration data sets from multiple channels in distributed architecture. Although concepts of Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) seemed promising in data analysis, their practical implementation revealed numerous problems. Generally, ML methods require a priori selection of fault-sensitive features, while DL methods require high-fidelity training sets. Plus, both techniques typically show results in newly generated, frequently abstract domain, which is of additional difficulty within interpretation. The presented method aims in overcoming listed obstacles. It shows how to take advantage of relative differences in data distribution for the purpose of anomaly detection, understood as machine fault detection (introduced in literature recently as “ajab” classifier). The method is designed to process relatively large data sets, without training sets. The method performance is demonstrated on real data.

title: Revolutionizing IoT Connectivity with Low-Cost Wide-Range LPWAN Technology Sigfox
abstract: This article focuses on the benefits and practical implementation of a prototype IoT system utilizing the Sigfox protocol and modern cloud services, tailored for the Industry 4.0 environment. The work explores the integration of low-power wide-area network (LPWAN) technologies with IoT systems to address challenges in efficiency, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. By leveraging Microsoft Azure components such as IoT Hub, Stream Analytics, and Power BI, the proposed solution demonstrates seamless device communication, real-time data analysis, and user-friendly visualization. Key advancements include a mobile application designed for smart home monitoring and control, utilizing sensors and actuators to create an intelligent environment. The use of Sigfox ensures energy-efficient, longrange connectivity, making the solution viable for industrial and consumer IoT applications alike. Additionally, the integration of Azure services enables scalability and adaptability to diverse IoT scenarios. The developed system provides a clear framework for future implementations in the Azure Industrial IoT ecosystem, offering robust solutions for monitoring, automation, and decision-making processes. This work underscores the transformative potential of combining LPWAN and cloud services in advancing smart IoT systems.

title: A Multi-Agent Approach to Investor Profiling Using Large Language Models
abstract: Investor profiling is essential in financial advising, allowing advisors to tailor investment strategies based on individual risk preferences, experience, and financial goals. This research aims to automate and enhance the investor profiling process using large language models (LLMs) through interactive multi-agent conversations. Our approach involves designing an investor agent, which represents a pre-defined investor derived from a narrative generated by a large language model (LLM) with given attributes, and an advisor agent that engages in conversation to infer the hidden attributes of the investor. The advisor-agent dynamically adjusts its questions based on previous conversation context to maximize the accuracy of its attribute predictions. The advisor agent makes predictions once it acquires sufficient information and compares them against the ground truth. We conducted extensive simulations across thousands of investor attribute sets and evaluated the effectiveness of the advisor-agent’s predictions based on key metrics. Our results demonstrate that LLM can effectively approximate investor characteristics. This research contributes to the field of AI-driven financial advising and unveils the potential of conversational agents in refining investor assessment methodologies.

title: Fault diagnosis in a HVAC monophase transmission line
abstract: This paper applies linear attention methods (LAMs) for creating models that recognize voltage sag, swell and harmonics in high voltage alternate current (HVAC) for a monophase transmission line. The method can simultaneously predict all the faults while working with various windows frames even as small as a half cycle. The approach is based on using data and allowing the model to learn the mathematical relationship between the input and the expected output by itself. The usage of LAMs made effective and precise predictions, in addition to the faster processing of information as compared with traditional attention models. The inclusion of different types of noises has not greatly affected the model’s performance, which produces a reliable method for identifying these types of distortions.

title: Dynamic Control of Servo-Hydraulics Inspired by Planetary Accretion
abstract: This paper introduces a control strategy for servohydraulic systems, inspired by the complex mechanical processes of Planet Formation. The proposed controller is designed around four fundamental physical principles that govern this phenomenon: gravitational forces acting in planets, drag forces towards planets, and matter accretion. To evaluate its effectiveness, the controller was tested using a nonlinear system model. The results highlight its performance in tracking various step trajectories, even under the influence of disturbances caused by unmodeled friction. Compared to conventional PID and Sliding Mode controllers, the proposed method achieved performance improvements of up to $46.85 \%$ and $49.75 \%$, respectively, based on four optimization criteria focused on minimizing energy consumption with trajectory constraints. This approach demonstrates significant potential for advancing the development of astrophysical-inspired control systems and can be extended to a variety of control system applications.

title: GRAPE: A Grid for Ranking and Analysis of Priority Environmental indicators in maintenance activities
abstract: The increasing environmental impact of industrial operations has emphasized the need for sustainable practices in maintenance activities. This paper introduces GRAPE (Grid for Ranking and Analysis of Priority Environmental indicators), a systematic evaluation framework designed to identify, classify, and prioritize environmental indicators associated with maintenance processes. By integrating environmental, operational, and transportation-related impacts, the GRAPE framework assesses indicators using three key criteria: importance, measurability, and urgency. The result is a quantifiable Environmental Impact Priority Level (EIPL), facilitating targeted decision-making and preventive strategies. A case study on distributed maintenance demonstrates the applicability of GRAPE, highlighting high-priority indicators and suggesting optimization actions such as route planning, stock sizing, and water treatment. The GRAPE grid offers a practical tool for aligning maintenance strategies with environmental sustainability goals..

title: Impact of Packet Errors on the Min-Consensus Protocol: General Analysis and Application to an Industrial Use Case
abstract: Joint decision-making is essential in multi-agent systems when autonomous entities want to collaborate to perform a joint task. The consensus protocol is a viable algorithm for enabling agents to reach a common agreement through communication. Achieving a fast consensus is crucial to ensure timely decision-making. However, communication errors, such as packet errors, can occur, potentially delaying the consensus-reaching process. Moreover, energy consumption remains a challenge in wireless communication. Reducing transmission power can decrease energy consumption but leads to higher packet error rates. In this paper, we investigate the impact of packet errors on the min-consensus protocol with application to an industrial use case. Using simulations of a mobile robot network, we evaluate how varying packet error rates influence the convergence speed, providing insights into the min-consensus protocol’s reliability and efficiency in the presence of packet errors.

title: On-Site Unmodeled Process Forces Integration in AMB Turboexpander Control
abstract: High-performance controllers are essential for safe operation of turbomachinery equipped with active magnetic bearings (AMBs). Although rotor models can be highly accurate-particularly when updated using factory frequency response data-discrepancies still occur between these models and real-world, on-site measurements. These discrepancies are primarily attributed to the influence of fluids, which significantly affect rotor dynamics. In this work, on-site measurements obtained via AMBs are exploited to estimate the unmodeled aerodynamic forces acting on the turbomachinery impellers by analysing the frequency response of the on-site process. By utilizing an updated rotor model, the process frequency responses related to aerodynamic forces can be identified and subsequently estimated. Once these aerodynamic forces are estimated, they are integrated into the control design. This integration allows for the optimization of the current supplied to the magnetic bearings, ensuring the system avoids actuator saturation and operates within safe limits. This approach significantly enhances overall plant performance by improving stability and safety, even when complex aerodynamic forces are difficult to model explicitly.

title: Flatness-Based Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics Utilizing Algebraic Differentiators
abstract: Deriving models from measurement data is a task encountered in multiple scientific disciplines. Many modern approaches tend to utilize the increasing computational capacity to build up a model from data. In contrast, a very efficient approach utilizing algebraic differentiators and least-squares optimization to derive a system model is proposed. The result of the identification process is a non-linear differentially flat system model. In addition to the model parameters, the presented approach identifies the structure of the system model. To limit the considered function space whilst enabling the adaptation of model complexity, an iterative procedure and an adaptation method of the function space is proposed. To illustrate the procedure, the system identification of a drive unit of a cable-driven parallel robot is presented.

title: Implementation of Machine Learning Algorithms to Forecast Noise Disturbances in the Surrounding Area of Monastir International Airport, Tunisia
abstract: Aviation-induced noise pollution represents a significant issue for populations residing in proximity to airports. Elevated noise levels, predominantly associated with aircraft take-off and landing operations, exert multiple adverse effects on the quality of life of nearby inhabitants. This research focuses on the systematic analysis of aircraft noise through the acquisition of acoustic data in the vicinity of Monastir International Airport. The primary objective is to construct a predictive model utilizing machine learning algorithms to estimate noise levels. The proposed model integrates various independent variables, including the distance between the noise source and the measurement device, atmospheric pressure, wind speed and direction, aircraft type, and passenger count. During the modelling process, multiple machine learning algorithms were evaluated, with hyperparameters optimized to enhance predictive accuracy. A comprehensive performance assessment of the developed models based on evaluation metrics identified the random forest algorithm as the most suitable approach, exhibiting superior predictive capabilities and overall performance.

title: Pole optimization of Bilinear-Laguerre model using PSO method: Application to the 2-DOF helicopter system
abstract: This paper presents the pole optimization of the Bilinear-Laguerre model using the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) method. The optimization process aims to improve the dynamic performance of the system by adjusting the pole locations, thereby enhancing the accuracy and stability of the model. The approach is validated through simulations on a 2 Degree-Of-Freedom helicopter system. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the PSO method in obtaining optimal pole placements, which lead to improved system behavior and performance.

title: Detection of Drone and Non-Drone Objects with Machine Learning Methods Using Visual Data
abstract: Nowadays, with the development of technology, the use of drones has increased in different areas. It is used effectively in many areas, especially defense, shopping, exploration, photography, entertainment, education and health. This technology is used in processes that are beneficial to humanity, as well as in undesirable situations that may harm humanity. Image classification is done successfully using deep learning methods. It is aimed to detect drone and non-drone objects from the image datasets given in this article using image processing methods. For this purpose, the train folder was used in the data folder in the data set, and among the 901 images in this folder, 502 images with drones and 399 images without drones were used. Images were classified with Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) learning methods using SqueezeNet and InceptionV3 architectures. Each image was classified with Artificial Neural Network (ANN), K Nearest Neighbor (KNN), Random Forest (RF), AdaBoost (AB), Decision Tree (DT) machine learning models found in SqueezeNet and InceptionV3 models. Cross validation method was used to evaluate the performance of the models. Accuracy, AUC, precision, F1 Score, recall and specificity metrics were used to measure the performance of the models. In the SqueezeNet model, classification success was achieved with ANN $91 \%$, AB $79 \%$, KNN 88.8%, DT 81.4% and RF 84.2% rates. In the classification made with InceptionV3, classification success was achieved with ANN 94.9%, AB 82.8%, KNN 92.6%, DT 84.4% and RF 88.5%. The study contributes to its use in real life, especially in detecting enemy targets in defense technologies, separating foreign plants from normal plants in agriculture, and many other areas.

title: Intelligent Purchasing Logistics: A Catalyst for Technological Performance and A Strategic Lever for Future Supply Chains
abstract: Within a technological and sustainable context, companies are paying increasing attention to the intelligence of their activities, from purchasing logistics to downstream. This awareness has placed the evaluation of smart purchasing performance at the heart of their operational strategies. To highlight these logistics, our study proposes an innovative multi-criteria evaluation methodology, based on a detailed breakdown of the purchasing sub-processes. This approach makes it possible to identify relevant indicators reflecting its multidimensional performance. Based on Principal Component Analysis, we have been able to highlight levers for improvement, thereby strengthening the competitiveness of companies. This methodology supports the alignment of operations with sustainable development principles and social, environmental and technological expectations.

title: Real-time Detection and Tracking of Wasps Using Quadcopter
abstract: This study presents the first attempt at a real time autonomous system for detecting and tracking wasps using a quad-rotor platform. The detection process leverages the YOLOv8 algorithm, a state-of-the-art deep learning method, which is trained on a custom dataset and implemented in Python. Images captured by the quad-rotor’s camera are processed to detect the target, and these are analyzed by the YOLOv8 model. Upon detection, the position of the wasp is identified, and tracking errors are computed, which are then used as input to a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) controller for real-time tracking. The conventional PID controller is employed to manage the quad-rotor’s speed and track the moving target. Controller gains (K ‘s) tuning was done using a trial-and-error approach. Indoor experiments with an ARDrone demonstrate the system’s ability to accurately detect and track wasps in real time. This validates the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, proving the possibility for future developments.

title: Fractional-Order Unknown Input Observer for Robust State Estimation in Dynamic Systems: Application to Battery Management
abstract: Accurate state estimation is essential for monitoring and controlling dynamic systems, especially in the presence of unknown inputs such as disturbances, modelling uncertainties, and sensor noise. Conventional state observers often struggle under these conditions, leading to biased or inaccurate estimations. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a fractional-order unknown input observer (UIO) that integrates fractional-order calculus with state augmentation techniques to enhance estimation robustness and precision. The proposed observer systematically incorporates unknown inputs into the estimation process by augmenting the system model, thereby mitigating their adverse effects. Fractional-order dynamics are leveraged to capture memory effects and hereditary system properties, offering superior adaptability compared to conventional integer-order observers. A rigorous stability analysis based on Lyapunov theory and Linear Matrix Inequalities (LMI) ensures asymptotic convergence of estimation errors. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, numerical simulations are conducted on a lithium-ion battery system, a widely used energy storage technology in electric vehicles and renewable energy applications. The results demonstrate that the proposed observer provides high estimation accuracy, effectively rejects disturbances, and improves robustness compared to conventional estimation techniques. The proposed methodology has broad applicability in autonomous systems, robotics, power electronics, and industrial process control, where real-time state estimation under uncertainty is critical. Future research will focus on real-time implementation, hardware validation, and extending the framework to nonlinear and multi-cell battery systems to enhance practical applicability.

title: Gesture Recognition for Mechatronic System Control Using Motion-Capture Suit Rokoko SmartSuit Pro II
abstract: This study explores the processing and evaluation of data collected from a motion-capture suit to facilitate gesture recognition for controlling mechatronic devices. In the context of advanced human-machine interaction (HMI) and human-robot interaction (HRI), the research investigates the utilization of motion-capture technology as a tool for capturing and interpreting operator gestures. The study begins with the selection of a cost-effective motion-capture suit, followed by a comprehensive analysis of data acquisition techniques. A program is then designed to process and evaluate the captured data using computational intelligence methods, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) networks. The results demonstrate the efficacy of these models in accurately recognizing gestures, which are then translated into control commands for mechatronic devices. This work contributes to the development of more intuitive and efficient interfaces for human-machine communication, with potential applications in various fields, including robotics, healthcare, and industrial automation.

title: Data-Driven Supervision of a Thermal-Hydraulic Process Towards a Physics-Based Digital Twin
abstract: The real-time supervision of production processes is a common challenge across several industries. It targets process component monitoring and its predictive maintenance in order to ensure safety, uninterrupted production and maintain high efficiency level. The rise of advanced tools for the simulation of physical systems in addition to data-driven machine learning models offers the possibility to design numerical tools dedicated to efficient system monitoring. In that respect, the digital twin concept presents an adequate framework that proffers solution to these challenges. The main purpose of this paper is to develop such a digital twin dedicated to fault detection and diagnosis in the context of a thermal-hydraulic process supervision. Based on a numerical simulation of the system, in addition to machine learning methods, we propose different modules dedicated to process parameter change detection and their on-line estimation. The proposed fault detection and diagnosis algorithm is validated on a specific test scenario, with single one-off parameter change occurrences in the system. The numerical results show good accuracy in terms of parameter variation localization and the update of their values.
tldr: A digital twin dedicated to fault detection and diagnosis in the context of a thermal-hydraulic process supervision is proposed and validated on a specific test scenario, with single one-off parameter change occurrences in the system.

title: Graph Attention Network-based EEG Classification Technology using Functional Brain Connectivity Information
abstract: Graphs serve as an effective means to represent complex systems. The interactions of brain waves can be represented by such graphs. The Mirror Neuron System (MNS) comprises a network of neurons that activate not only during action execution but also when observing the same actions performed by other individuals or entities. Due to its characteristics of simultaneous activation during imitation learning, mental imagery, and movement, the MNS plays a critical role in intention recognition. Electroencephalogram (EEG) data analysis emphasizes the importance of connectivity information between electrodes. In this study, raw EEG data were preprocessed using min-max normalization, followed by application of an $8-13 \mathrm{~Hz}$ bandpass filter and Fast Independent Component Analysis (Fast ICA). The processed data were utilized to train a Graph Attention Network (GAT) for classifying relationships between electrode signals. The first layer of the GAT incorporated a functional brain connectivity-based Informed Network to constrain relationships, enabling the model to learn both time-based and frequencybased connections. Considering the characteristics of EEG data, classification results were analyzed individually for each subject, followed by comparative analysis of brain connectivity patterns.

title: Monitoring Turbine Generator Sets with a Hybrid Unsupervised Learning Strategy
abstract: Gas and steam turbines play a critical role in ensuring power grid stability, particularly as the share of variable renewable energy sources like wind and solar continues to increase. As essential components of modern thermal power systems, gas and steam turbines offer significant operational flexibility, allowing them to rapidly adjust output in response to real-time fluctuations in grid demand. Several industrial applications have demonstrated that Self-Organizing Maps (SOMs) provide substantial benefits in anomaly detection, as well as in identifying and visualizing process phases, thus facilitating comprehensive monitoring and improved understanding of technical processes. To address the limitations associated with SOMs, particularly when dealing with imbalanced datasets and strongly correlated process variables, this article evaluates a hybrid unsupervised learning strategy (HULS) for monitoring a turbine generator set.

title: Comparative Study in Fault Detection under Data Scarcity for Pharmaceutical Device Manufacturing
abstract: Developing a data-driven fault detection model for an actual manufacturing process poses significant challenges, particularly during the design phase, when accessing a large volume of data is not feasible. One approach to address data scarcity is the generation of synthetic datasets to aid in the training process. This paper focuses on conducting a comparative study between deep generative models and a simulation model for generating synthetic samples. Various deep generative models such as Variational Autoencoder (VAE) are explored and applied in this study. Additionally, the Finite Element Analysis (FEA) technique is employed to develop the simulation model, leveraging physicsbased approaches. Furthermore, the performance of these generative techniques is evaluated and compared using qualitative and quantitative evaluation methods. The results demonstrate that different methods exhibit varying levels of performance based on qualitative and quantitative criteria. Eventually, the impact of synthetic samples generated by different approaches on the fault detection model is evaluated and reported. Synthetic normal and anomalous samples are added to the training set, and the model is tested solely on real data. The results indicate that majority of generative techniques enhance the performance of the fault detection model, requiring fewer training iterations and improving the model’s predictive capabilities. Notably, synthetic data generated by VAE significantly improve the fault detection model’s F1 score when only synthetic normal samples added to the training set by 0.185 in our case.

title: On the optimization of NARX-Laguerre model. Application to 1-DoF AERO drone
abstract: This paper introduces NARX-Laguerre modeling, a method for optimizing and reducing NARX model parameters. It first explains the NARX model and proposes a criterion that balances complexity and error for parameter optimization. To achieve reduction, the parameters are projected onto five independent Laguerre bases, resulting in a simplified representation of nonlinear systems. The process of obtaining Laguerre functions, designing filters, and applying them to variables is detailed and formulated into algorithms. The proposed modeling approach is efficiently applied to a real 1-DoF drone.

title: Control of Warehouse System Model Using PLC and Multiplatform Mobile Application: Educational Case Study for IT and Automation Convergence
abstract: The digitalisation of production systems under Industry 4.0 emphasizes the convergence of information (IT) and operational technologies (OT) to enhance industrial processes and efficiency. This work addresses the educational gap in ITOT integration through a case study demonstrating the control of a virtual warehouse system using a programmable logic controller (PLC) and a mobile application. The study employs accessible tools like Codesys for PLC programming, Factory I/O for warehouse system simulation, and Node-RED for middleware communication, leveraging OPC UA and MQTT protocols for seamless data exchange. Operators can manage and monitor the system via a mobile interface developed using multiplatform framework, enabling both automated and manual control modes. This case study offers a practical framework for teaching Industry 4.0 concepts, bridging IT and OT, and advancing digitalisation in industrial automation.

title: Overview of Sensors and Signals in Structural Health Monitoring: A Case Study on Crack Detection in a Ship’s Hull*
abstract: Sensing is an important step in structural health monitoring (SHM) since it provides good information to be analyzed by suitable signal processing techniques. This overview discusses several key sensors and the importance of proper sensor placement. Moreover, it presents important research on signal processing approaches for SHM. This overview aims to shed light on the importance of signals’ sensing and processing in SHM. It delves into a case study on crack detection in a ship’s hull using a methodology based on power spectral density. Real industrial data is collected using long-base strain gauge (LBSG) sensors positioned on the ship’s structure. The findings reveal no evidence of fatigue cracks in the ship’s structure.

title: Real-Time Traffic Analysis: Data processing using MQTT and InfluxDB
abstract: Cooperative Intelligent Transport Systems (C-ITS) enhance road safety, traffic flow, and accident prevention through advanced communication technologies. V2X (Vehicle-toEverything) communication enables vehicles to exchange realtime data with other vehicles (V2V), infrastructure (V2I), and pedestrians (V2P), creating an adaptive network. However, managing and processing this vast real-time data remains a significant challenge. This paper addresses the problem of accurately transcribing and processing critical road data, such as vehicle speed, direction, and angle, to enhance traffic management and accident prevention. By refining Cooperative Perception Message (CPM) generation and analysis, the system dynamically classifies traffic as inflow or outflow near Road-Side Units (RSUs), improving accuracy and contributing to a safer, more responsive intelligent transportation ecosystem. The proposed system is capable of processing real-time road data to extract critical information regarding vehicle speeds and directional movements. These insights contribute to the development of more responsive and adaptive traffic management strategies, enhancing the overall safety and efficiency of road networks. By facilitating timely data exchanges and rapid decision-making, this system represents a significant step towards achieving smarter and safer transportation ecosystems.

title: Wavelet Images and Joint Deep-Machine Learning for Anomaly Detection in Airbus Helicopters Exclusively with Healthy Accelerometer Data
abstract: Anomaly detection in aircraft systems, such as helicopters, plays a critical role in ensuring operational safety and reliability. A primary challenge in this domain is the unavailability of sufficient labeled data, with the majority of available datasets comprising only healthy conditions. This paper addresses these challenges by proposing a robust pipeline for anomaly detection using accelerometer data collected from Airbus helicopter systems. Specifically, the accelerometer data, which often exhibits complex spatiotemporal characteristics, is first transformed into images using Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT). Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is then applied to reduce the dimensionality of the data while retaining the maximum variance (i.e., $100 \%$), thereby compressing it with minimal loss. Subsequently, a Fast AutoEncoder (FAE), trained using a pseudo-inverse method on healthy data, is employed to predict anomalies. The prediction errors are analyzed through several small-scale machine learning models, including Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM), Support Vector Machines (SVM), and AutoEncoders trained with Stochastic Gradient Descent (AESGD). To optimize performance, Bayesian optimization is integrated for hyperparameter tuning, including threshold-based classification. Key data visualizations are presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of CWT transformation, the quality of the data processed by FAE, and the potential for separating data patterns. Finally, performance comparisons based on ROC, AUC, and classification threshold demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed pipeline, with SVM achieving an AUC of 0.83281, which is $55.46 \%$ higher than GMM and 7.28% higher than AESGD. This highlights its superior ability to separate anomalous patterns compared to other methods.

title: Detection and diagnosis in the operation and management of industrial processes
abstract: The standard evolution of an industrial process respects certain operating domains defined by technological limitations. When the operating domain limit is exceeded the evolution of the plant is not normal. Detection and diagnosis are actions that contribute to keeping safety in the exploitation of the process. The paper presents detection and diagnosis concepts based on the dependency and the causal connections that exist between the process parameters and proposes efficient techniques and algorithms for the safe use of the process. In order to detect the process faults, the paper proposes a method based on residue evaluation using the measurements collected from the process and on the process associated mathematical equations. The faults causes are estimated through a diagnosis arborescent structure organized as a causal graph. The variables and connections associated with the graph are determined from the a-priori knowledge about the process.

title: Portable EEG-Based Data Acquisition and Multi-Sensor Integration Using the Muse S Headband
abstract: Wearable EEG headsets play an important role in brain-computer interfaces, cognitive experimentation, and mental health diagnostics. However, most commercial devices, including the InteraXon Muse S, are hindered by proprietary software limitations, single-user capability, or dependence on specific third-party mobile applications. The present paper proposes a solution we call “MuseCroc”, an Internet of Things (IoT) solution based on Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) technology, for automated real-time EEG data collection, processing, and diagnostics. MuseCroc facilitates low-latency, high-fidelity data streaming and multi-device synchronization. With the addition of edge storage through SD cards, it offers a stable, portable, and scalable EEG data acquisition solution without the need for proprietary software. This strategy improves the usability of consumer EEG devices for large-scale research studies, real-time Brain Computer Interface (BCI), and automated neurological monitoring. MuseCroc’s modular nature renders it a versatile platform for researchers and developers in biomedical signal processing and BCIs.

title: Bibliometric Analysis on the Application of Quantitative Decision-Aiding Methods to the Decarbonization of Manufacturing Processes
abstract: Decarbonization has become a crucial imperative in the industrial sector due to its significant contribution to global greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. This study conducts a comprehensive bibliometric analysis of academic research on decarbonization in manufacturing processes and operations management. Using a systematic literature review approach, 105 relevant papers were identified from an initial pool of $\mathbf{8, 0 3 2}$ publications retrieved from the Web of Science database. The analysis categorizes research contributions based on key operations management topics, like production planning, production scheduling, process design, and inventory management, while also examining the main decarbonization focus, such as carbon reduction, energy reduction, waste reduction, and the predominant quantitative decision-aiding methods. Findings reveal that production planning is the most extensively studied area, with many papers directly considering carbon reduction, while methods such as mathematical modeling dominate decision-aiding methods. The study also identifies research gaps, particularly the underutilization of machine learning and multi-criteria decision-making (MCDM) in decarbonization efforts and offers recommendations for futures research.

title: Modeling and Simulation of Trajectory Tracking for a Reconfigurable Serial Drone Using Simscape Multibody
abstract: In recent years, several classes of transformable drones have emerged, including a novel category known as serial drones. These systems have garnered increasing attention due to their structural adaptability and their ability to perform complex missions such as aerial manipulation and object transportation. Their serial architecture, actuated by servomotors between the arms, enables precise morphological reconfiguration, allowing agile navigation in cluttered or constrained environments. To accurately capture the dynamic behavior of such drones, especially during reconfiguration, a comprehensive and parameter sensitive model is essential. This paper introduces a generic dynamic modeling framework for transformable aerial drones, incorporating variations in key inertial properties namely the inertia matrix, Center of Gravity (CoG), and control allocation matrix throughout the transformation process. A detailed multibody simulation model is also developed using Simscape Multibody (SM) and integrated with a Computer Aided Design (CAD) of the reconfigurable drone. A Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) controller is synthesized and applied identically to both the analytical and multibody models to ensure consistent performance evaluation. Simulation results reveal a strong coherence between the two approaches and confirm the accuracy of the proposed dynamic model. Furthermore, the effectiveness of the control strategy is demonstrated through stable trajectory tracking, validating the framework as a solid foundation for the development of advanced aerial robotics systems.

title: Time-Frequency Analysis and Deep CNN-Based Fault Diagnosis for Rotating Machinery Under Variable Operating Conditions
abstract: Fault diagnosis in rotating machinery is essential for ensuring the reliability and operational efficiency of industrial systems. As machinery systems become increasingly complex, effective fault diagnosis becomes challenging. Data-driven approaches have gained prominence because of their ability to handle large datasets. However, traditional machine learning methods often rely on manual feature extraction, which is timeconsuming and less adaptable to varying fault conditions than deep learning methods. This study proposes an automated and robust fault diagnosis system capable of accurately detecting complex machinery faults, including combined faults under variable operating conditions. The framework transforms machinery vibration signals into 2D time-frequency representations (TFRs) using continuous wavelet transform (CWT), preserving critical temporal and spectral fault signatures, which are then processed through a convolutional neural network (CNN) architecture for automated feature learning and pattern recognition. Experimental validation using the Machinery Fault Simulator at the Complex Systems and Interactions (CSI) laboratory demonstrated the high accuracy of the CNN model in challenging scenarios involving variable operations and complex combined faults.

title: Virtual Reality in Industrial Training: A Case Study on Distillery Operator Education
abstract: This study explores the application of virtual reality (VR) in industrial settings, specifically focusing on the training of distillery operators. VR offers unique advantages, such as cost-effective and safe training environments, enabling users to practice complex tasks without real-world risks. The project aimed to develop a VR application that simulates realistic distillery processes and equipment, providing users with hands-on experience in a controlled environment. The application was built using advanced 3D modeling and simulation techniques to replicate key operational tasks, such as valve manipulation, equipment monitoring, and process optimization. Interactive elements, including operational manuals and alarm systems, were incorporated to enhance usability. Additionally, random event scenarios, such as equipment malfunctions, were introduced to challenge trainees and improve decision-making under pressure. The system supports both immersive VR and desktop environments, ensuring accessibility and scalability for diverse training needs. This work demonstrates the potential of VR to revolutionize industrial training by improving safety, efficiency, and user engagement. Future development will focus on extending functionality, incorporating artificial intelligence for adaptive training, and exploring its applications in other industrial domains.

title: Cybersecurity - Enhancing Digital Transaction Authentication through Improved Digital Signature Process
abstract: Digital signatures are crucial for ensuring the authenticity, integrity, and non-repudiation of digital messages, especially in business applications where data security is paramount. This paper explores improvements to the digital signature process, focusing on the RSA algorithm and its integration with the SHA-256 hashing algorithm. By employing variations in the RSA algorithm, such as using different numbers of prime factors (two and four primes), we aim to enhance the security and efficiency of digital signatures. The research is divided into four scenarios: H2PN, H4PN, A2PN, and A4PN, each differing in the number of prime factors used and whether the hash value is signed directly or converted to ASCII before signing. The effectiveness of these scenarios is evaluated using texts of varying lengths, from short phrases to longer passages, to measure the avalanche effect—the change in the output when a single bit of the input is altered. Experimental results demonstrate that increasing the number of prime factors generally enhances the security of the digital signatures by increasing the avalanche effect. The proposed methods are compared with traditional RSA and other cryptographic algorithms, highlighting improvements in both security and performance. Our findings suggest that converting the hash to an alphanumerical format (ASCII) before encryption significantly improves the avalanche effect, enhancing the security and robustness of the digital signatures. Each scenario is tested multiple times to ensure accuracy, and the results demonstrate that using four prime numbers and alphanumerical conversion yields the best performance in terms of avalanche effect and overall security. In conclusion, this study provides valuable insights into optimizing digital signatures using advanced RSA configurations, offering a practical solution for enhanced data security in digital communications.

title: KoGPT-2 Integrated SSVEP-Based BCI for Efficient Language Processing
abstract: Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) is a technology that analyzes neural signals to enable interaction with machines, showing promising potential as an innovative communication tool, especially for individuals with language impairments. Recent studies have explored various methods of utilizing BCI systems to assist people who have lost their language abilities. Among these, the BCI system called Mind Speller has been employed to evaluate user intent recognition technology while simultaneously supporting practical communication. The SSVEP (Steady-State Visual Evoked Potential)-based Mind Speller is designed to analyze brainwave responses generated when users focus on visual stimuli, allowing them to input intended characters. However, the input speed of the existing Mind Speller system is still slower than conventional keyboard typing, necessitating more efficient methods for real-time communication. Additionally, Korean Mind Speller has not been actively researched due to the complexity of Hangul’s initial, medial, and final consonants, which make typing slower compared to English. However, in this study, we proposeed a solution to this problem by utilizing an autocomplete system based on initial consonant search. Furthermore, this study proposes integrating the Mind Speller system with Large Language Model (LLM) technology. Specifically, by combining the Korean language model KoGPT2 with Mind Speller, the goal is to enhance input speed and generate more natural and accurate sentences. KoGPT-2 demonstrates outstanding performance in Korean text generation and contextual understanding, offering autocomplete and sentence prediction functionalities, making it a valuable tool to overcome the limitations of BCI-based typing systems.

title: Machine Learning and Word Representation Techniques in Medical Transcription Data Analysis
abstract: Dermatology is the branch of medicine that deals with the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of skin diseases. Dermatological diseases can be difficult to diagnose, treat, and manage because there are several skin conditions, each with its unique set of symptoms and causes. Underlying medical conditions, environmental causes, or hereditary characteristics can cause complex skin problems. Furthermore, because skin problems can present in a variety of ways, obtaining an appropriate diagnosis and efficient treatment may be difficult. Treating dermatological disorders is a difficult endeavor. This article proposes an integrated model to assist people in understanding and discussing the nature of dermatology. This model’s capabilities include text pre-processing, audio-to-text translation, named entity recognition (NER) for extracting crucial information, and text clustering and classification based on content. The necessity for precise and efficient analysis of large amounts of text data, notably the identification and standardisation of abbreviations and the extraction of relevant information, has been identified as a problem in dermatology and medical transcription. By grouping similar cases, clustering can make it easier to spot patterns and trends in dermatological disorders. However, classification can help automatically group text data into pre-established categories, such as various kinds of skin conditions or treatments. These methods simplify data analysis, increase accuracy, and assist healthcare professionals in reaching accurate conclusions regarding patient care. This article explores the partitioning algorithm for clustering, while logistic regression is used in classification. The model analysed in this article helps dermatologists and patients understand and manage skin problems

title: Revolutionizing IoT Connectivity with Low-Cost Wide-Range LPWAN Technology Sigfox
abstract: This article focuses on the benefits and practical implementation of a prototype IoT system utilizing the Sigfox protocol and modern cloud services, tailored for the Industry 4.0 environment. The work explores the integration of low-power wide-area network (LPWAN) technologies with IoT systems to address challenges in efficiency, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. By leveraging Microsoft Azure components such as IoT Hub, Stream Analytics, and Power BI, the proposed solution demonstrates seamless device communication, real-time data analysis, and user-friendly visualization. Key advancements include a mobile application designed for smart home monitoring and control, utilizing sensors and actuators to create an intelligent environment. The use of Sigfox ensures energy-efficient, longrange connectivity, making the solution viable for industrial and consumer IoT applications alike. Additionally, the integration of Azure services enables scalability and adaptability to diverse IoT scenarios. The developed system provides a clear framework for future implementations in the Azure Industrial IoT ecosystem, offering robust solutions for monitoring, automation, and decision-making processes. This work underscores the transformative potential of combining LPWAN and cloud services in advancing smart IoT systems.

title: Bibliometric Analysis of Emerging Trends and Future Directions in AI-Driven Fault Diagnosis for Photovoltaic Systems Using Advanced Image Processing Techniques
abstract: Traditional fault detection methods in photovoltaic systems face limitations when handling emerging monitoring systems that generate vast amounts of high-dimensional data across various domains. Consequently, there is growing interest in the international scientific community in applying artificial intelligence (AI) methods, which offer a promising solution for effectively managing large datasets and detecting faults. This paper aims to assess the current state of AI-based fault diagnosis in photovoltaic systems through a bibliometric analysis. A total of 439 papers published between 2008 and March 2025 were analyzed using data from the Web of Science. The study identifies key trends, influential authors, leading journals and countries, prominent citations, collaborative networks, and keyword co-occurrence that shape the field. Overall, the most recurring topics in AI-based fault diagnosis for photovoltaic systems have been identified, offering insights into future research directions. These include the integration of advanced machine learning models, real-time monitoring techniques, predictive maintenance strategies, and the optimization of fault detection algorithms.

title: Intelligent Automated Feeding System for Lumber Quality Check in Industrialized Construction
abstract: Quality control is a crucial function in off-site construction, especially for maintaining the integrity of lumber materials. Natural wood defects, such as twists and crooks, can compromise structural strength, leading to production delays and increased material waste. This paper presents an intelligent system for real-time defect detection and prediction in lumber. The system integrates the YOLOv8 model (for efficient wood defect detection) with cubic spline interpolation (for precise defect localization and decision-making). With a precision of $99.03 \%$ and a recall of $100 \%$, the proposed system significantly reduces rework, material waste, and production delays. By providing accurate and timely defect information, this system supports effective quality control and enhances the overall efficiency of the manufacturing process in off-site wood construction. The system’s ability to predict defects ensures the production of highquality wood components for modular construction, improving operational workflows and reducing waste.

title: SpineNet: Automated Detection and Classification of Lumbar Spine Disorders Using Multi-Orientation MRI and Deep Learning
abstract: This study introduces a novel hybrid deep learning approach that integrates ResNet-50 and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for the automated classification of lumbar spine degenerative disorders. Leveraging a comprehensive dataset of 1,975 patients from the RSNA 2024 Lumbar Spine Degenerative Classification challenge, our model processes multiorientation MRI sequences to classify 25 different degenerative conditions across three severity levels. The proposed architecture utilizes ResNet-50 for spatial feature extraction and LSTM for capturing temporal dependencies, achieving AUC scores of 0.82, 0.79, and 0.88 for Normal/Mild, Moderate, and Severe classifications, respectively. Our method incorporates advanced preprocessing techniques and data augmentation strategies, including brightness adjustment, shear transformation, and scaling, to enhance model robustness. The model demonstrates promising results, there is potential for further optimization through hyperparameter tuning and increased training data. This work contributes to the development of automated diagnostic support systems in spine imaging, offering potential clinical value in improving diagnostic accuracy and efficiency.

title: Cryptanalysis and Security Evaluation of a Fast Image Encryption Algorithm Based on the Modified Logistic Map
abstract: Ensuring the security of digital images is crucial in various domains, including medical imaging, secure communications, and military applications. Recently, a fast image encryption algorithm based on a substitution-permutation network and a modified logistic map has been proposed, claiming to offer enhanced security and computational efficiency. The encryption process follows three main phases: permutation, substitution, and XOR operation with a pseudo-random binary sequence. However, in this study, we conduct a rigorous cryptanalysis of the algorithm and demonstrate its susceptibility to chosen-plaintext attacks. By systematically reversing each encryption phase, we develop an efficient method capable of recovering the original image with a computational complexity of $O(N)$, where N represents the image size. The experimental results confirm the feasibility of our attack, revealing fundamental weaknesses in the algorithm’s security mechanisms. Our findings indicate that the encryption scheme fails to provide the necessary cryptographic robustness required for protecting sensitive image data. This study underscores the importance of comprehensive security evaluations in cryptographic algorithm design and highlights the need for more resilient encryption schemes to withstand modern attack vectors.

title: Optimization of intermittent androgen suppression therapy for prostate cancer using PSO algorithm*
abstract: Intermittent Androgen Suppression (IAS) therapy is of interest in metastatic prostate cancer due to its ability to reduce side effects compared with Continuous Androgen (CAS) therapy, while improving patient’s life expectancy. However, the main challenge lies in determining the optimal treatment intervals (on/off phases) to improve its performance. Since disease progression is inevitable in metastatic cases, the goal is to maximize the time to tumor recurrence (delaying androgen resistance) and minimize the side effects of therapy. To solve this problem, this work proposes the use of the Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm to determine the optimal treatment intervals for IAS therapy. The approach is based on a mathematical model that describes the interaction between androgens and tumor cell populations, incorporating Prostate-Specific Antigen (PSA) thresholds as critical parameters in the therapeutic strategy. The numerical results obtained show that therapy with on/off intervals determined through PSO allows obtaining satisfactory results.

title: Digital Tourism Recommendation System Based on Harmony Search Algorithm
abstract: The advancement of virtual reality (VR) technology and metaverse technology has led to an increase in digital tourism content. Digital tourism has established itself as a service that provides users with an immersive tourism experience without time and space constraints. However, the ability to instantly move between various tourist destinations without physical distance limitations can have a dual impact on user immersion. In particular, when moving to highly recommended tourist spots, there is a possibility of reduced immersion if the transition is to an environment that is excessively different from the previous location. In this paper, we propose a new fitness function that considers the relevance between travel destinations and user immersion to enhance the immersiveness of digital tourism experiences. We apply the Harmony Search Algorithm (HSA), which can effectively solve complex optimization problems while maintaining a balance between exploration and exploitation. Based on this, we implement a digital tourism route planning provision system. Experimental results show that the proposed system achieves a $7 \%$ improvement in efficiency compared to genetic algorithms. Our research presents new possibilities for user satisfaction and route optimization in the field of digital tourism by providing users with a more continuous and consistent digital tourism experience.

title: Metaheuristic-Based Pole Optimization in NARX-Laguerre Model: An Approach Using Elephant Herding Algorithm
abstract: The NARX-Laguerre model employs a set of five separate Laguerre bases to parameterize the NARX model coefficients, resulting in an efficient and compact representation of nonlinear systems. Choosing the optimal Laguerre poles is crucial to the model’s performance since doing so significantly reduces the number of parameters required to run the system accurately while maintaining its accuracy. Here, we provide a bio-inspired metaheuristic method for estimating the best NARX-Laguerre poles from input-output data. In particular, we make use of the EHO algorithm, an optimization method derived from nature that is based on the idea of collective intelligence in elephant herds, to effectively explore and utilize the search area.
